title: Improve enum compatbility checks
body:
SystemVerilog requires that when assigning a value to an enum typed variable, that the assigned value is of the same enum type. This is currently enforced for direct assignments, but there are a few assignment like contexts where it is not. Such as function or task arguments or function return statements. E.g.

```SystemVerilog
typedef enum {A, B} E;
function E f(E e);
  return 10; // Should fail, but works
endfunction
initial f(10); // Should fail, but works
```
There are also some types of expressions that do not have their type attached to them. This will cause the enum compatibility check to fail, even when it should succeed. This is for struct members, class properties and array elements. E.g.

```SystemVerilog
typedef enum {A, B} E;
E e, a[2];
initial e = a[0]; // Should work, but fails
```

Make sure that all rvalue and lvalue expression where the type is known have their type properly attached to them. Then move the enum compatibility check into `elaborate_rval_expr()`, which is used to evaluate expressions for assignments and assignment-like contexts.

Having the type properly attached to expressions is also need for other constructs that need type information, such as assignment patterns.
